prompt:
It is 2026-04-08 14:30:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.

[CURRENT APPLIANCES STATUS]:
Livingroom:
Light has turned on
Fan set to 50%
PIR sensor motion detected
Temperature sensor: 31.2°C

Hallway:
Left light has turned off
Right light has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Main door set to 100% (fully open)
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor: 29.8°C

Bedroom + balcony:
Room light has turned off
Bed light has turned off
Balcony light outside has turned off
Fan set to 0%
Window curtain set to 50% (half open)
Lock has unlocked
Pump has turned off
PIR sensor no motion detected
Temperature sensor: 30.5°C
Temperature sensor outside: 33.1°C
Moisture sensor: 28.0%
eval_note: Correct if the agent identifies that: (1) livingroom is occupied (PIR motion + light on), temperatures are high across the house so fans may need adjusting, (2) main door is fully open with no hallway motion which is a security concern, (3) bedroom lock is unlocked with no one there, (4) moisture is low (28%) so bonsai may need watering. Should at minimum close the main door, lock the bedroom, and possibly water bonsai.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant S as ScheduleSession
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
S->>A: Overall check with open door, unlocked bedroom, low moisture
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>S: report security fixes and watering started

prompt: I just woke up and can barely see anything, it's still dark outside
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers the user is in the bedroom, just woke up and needs light. Should turn on bedroom room light or bed light. May also open curtain slightly. Should not turn on lights in other rooms since owner just woke up in bedroom.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Just woke up, dark outside
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: turned on bedroom light for morning

prompt: The air feels really stale and stuffy in here, I can barely breathe
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers the user needs better air circulation. Could ask which room, or check PIR sensors to determine occupied room. Should turn on fan in the occupied area or increase fan speed. May also suggest opening curtain/window.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Air feels stale and stuffy
A->>C: get espID=1 pir
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=3 pir
C-->>A: PIR values
alt motion in livingroom
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=70
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: increased fan for ventilation
else motion in bedroom
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=70
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=50
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: increased fan and opened curtain

prompt: I'm watching a scary movie and the balcony area keeps creeping me out with shadows
eval_note: Correct if the agent infers the user wants the balcony light turned on for comfort/safety, and possibly close curtain to block the view. User is likely in livingroom watching the movie.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Watching scary movie, balcony shadows creeping me out
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=true
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: balcony light on and curtain closed for comfort

prompt: Check the weather forecast for today. If it's going to rain, close the curtain and add a note for today saying 'bring laundry inside'. If it's sunny, open the curtain fully and water the bonsai only if moisture is below 40%.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) gets current date, (2) gets location, (3) gets weather forecast, (4) branches based on rain vs sunny - if rain: close curtain + add note; if sunny: open curtain + check moisture + conditionally water. Multi-tool, multi-step with weather-driven logic.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Weather-conditional curtain and bonsai action
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current date
A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(location, date)
W-->>A: forecast
alt rain expected
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>N: add_note(bring laundry inside, [today])
A-->>U: curtain closed and note added
else sunny
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=100
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
alt moisture < 40
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
A-->>U: curtain opened and watering started
else moisture >= 40
A-->>U: curtain opened, no watering needed

prompt: I need to prepare the house for a party tonight. Turn on all the lights in the livingroom and hallway, set fans to 60% everywhere, open the main door fully, and add a note for today saying 'party at home - check noise levels after 10pm'. Also schedule the agent to close the main door and reduce fans to 20% at 11pm.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) turns on livingroom light, hallway left and right lights, (2) sets all fans to 60%, (3) opens main door to 100%, (4) adds a note, (5) triggers schedule agent for 11pm actions. Comprehensive party setup combining appliances, notes, and scheduling.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant N as NoteTool
participant T as ScheduleTool
U->>A: Prepare house for party
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=60
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=60
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=60
C-->>A: success
A->>N: add_note(party at home - check noise levels after 10pm, [today])
N-->>A: note created
A->>T: trigger_schedule_agent(close door and reduce fans at 11pm)
T-->>A: schedule created
A-->>U: party setup complete with note and schedule

prompt: Read the current temperature in every room and outside. If any room is above 30°C, set that room's fan to 80%. If outside temperature is higher than inside bedroom, close the curtain. Then search the web for tips on cooling a room naturally and tell me the top 3 tips.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) reads all temperature sensors (espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem, espID=3 tem_out), (2) conditionally sets fans based on readings, (3) compares outdoor vs bedroom temp for curtain, (4) uses web search tool for cooling tips. Multi-step with sensors, conditional logic, and web search.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant W as WebTool
U->>A: Read temps, conditional fans, search cooling tips
A->>C: get espID=1 tem
A->>C: get espID=2 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: temperature values
alt any room > 30
A->>C: set espID=X motor1=80
C-->>A: success
end
alt outside > bedroom
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
end
A->>W: search_and_read_web_link(cooling room naturally)
W-->>A: search results
A-->>U: report temp actions and top 3 cooling tips

prompt: Check if anyone is in the hallway. If yes, turn on both hallway lights and open the main door. If no one is there, make sure the door is closed and lights are off.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads hallway PIR sensor first, then branches: motion detected → lights on + door open; no motion → lights off + door closed. Two-step conditional with sensor read then action.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Check hallway presence and act accordingly
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
C-->>A: PIR value
alt motion detected
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=100
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: hallway lit and door opened
else no motion
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: hallway dark and door closed

prompt: Read the moisture level of my bonsai. If it's below 30%, start watering and add a note for today saying 'watered bonsai at [current time]'. If it's above 60%, add a note saying 'bonsai moisture is high, skip watering'.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) reads moisture sensor, (2) gets current time, (3) branches based on moisture level with appropriate action and note. Should use get_current_datetime for timestamp in the note.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
participant T as TimeTool
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Check moisture and conditionally water with note
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current time
alt moisture < 30
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=true
A->>N: add_note(watered bonsai at time, [today])
A-->>U: watering started and note added
else moisture > 60
A->>N: add_note(bonsai moisture high skip watering, [today])
A-->>U: no watering needed, note added
else between 30-60
A-->>U: moisture is fine, no action needed

prompt: Get the temperature in the bedroom and outside. If the bedroom is hotter than outside by more than 3 degrees, open the curtain and set the fan to 50% to let cool air in. Otherwise keep the curtain closed.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) reads both bedroom temp and outdoor temp, (2) calculates or compares the difference, (3) if bedroom > outside + 3: open curtain + set fan; otherwise keep curtain closed. Proper conditional logic with two sensor readings.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Compare bedroom and outdoor temp for cooling
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: temperature values
alt bedroom > outside + 3
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=50
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: curtain opened and fan set for cooling
else difference <= 3
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: curtain kept closed

prompt: I smell gas in the hallway! What should I do?
eval_note: Correct if the agent treats this as an emergency: should NOT turn on/off any electrical devices that could cause sparks, should advise the owner to evacuate immediately, open the main door for ventilation (motor is safe as already powered), and call emergency services. Should NOT toggle lights or fans. Safety-first response.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Gas smell emergency in hallway
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=100
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: URGENT: evacuate immediately, door opened for ventilation, call emergency services, do not use electrical switches

prompt: The water pipe in the balcony burst! There's water everywhere outside!
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) immediately turns off the pump to stop additional water flow, (2) advises the owner to shut off the main water supply, (3) may turn on balcony light so user can see. Should act quickly and provide practical emergency advice.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Water pipe burst on balcony
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=true
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: pump stopped, balcony light on, advise shutting main water supply

prompt: I think someone is trying to break into the house! I can hear noises at the main door and I'm in the bedroom!
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) immediately locks the bedroom door (servo=true), (2) closes main door if open (motor2=0), (3) turns on all hallway lights to deter intruder, (4) checks hallway PIR for confirmation, (5) advises calling police. Security emergency with immediate protective actions.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Break-in attempt, user in bedroom
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=true
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=true
A->>C: get espID=2 pir
C-->>A: success and PIR value
A-->>U: bedroom locked, door secured, lights on to deter, call police immediately

prompt: I'm going on vacation for a week. Set up everything for maximum security and energy saving.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) turns off all lights, (2) turns off all fans, (3) closes main door fully, (4) locks bedroom, (5) closes curtain, (6) turns off pump. Everything should be secured and powered down. May also suggest adding a note or setting up motion-detection alerts.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Vacation mode - max security and energy saving
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led2=false
A->>C: set espID=3 led3=false
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=0
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=3 pump=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: full lockdown applied for vacation

prompt: I just left the house but forgot if I locked the bedroom. Check it and lock it if needed. Also make sure the main door is closed.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads the bedroom lock status (get servo) and hall door status (get motor2) first, then takes corrective action only if needed. Should not blindly set without checking first if the user wants to 'check'.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: get espID=2 motor2

alt not locked or door open
A->>C: set espID=3 servo=true
A->>C: set espID=2 motor2=0
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: secured both
else already secured
A-->>U: everything already locked and closed

prompt: Enable night mode for the house. I want only the bed light on at low brightness for comfort, everything else should be off and secured.
eval_note: Correct if the agent turns off all lights except bed light (led2 on), turns off all fans or sets bedroom fan to low, closes main door, locks bedroom, closes curtain. The user wants a minimal secure nighttime setup. Note: the system doesn't support dimming LEDs (only on/off), so bed light should just be turned on.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: set espID=3 led2=true

A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0

participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>C: get espID=2 pir
A->>C: get espID=3 pir
C-->>A: PIR values for all rooms
alt motion in specific rooms
A->>C: set lights on in occupied rooms only
C-->>A: success
end

prompt: Monitor the bedroom temperature. If it's between 25-28°C, set the fan to 30%. If it's between 28-32°C, set it to 60%. If it's above 32°C, set it to 100% and open the curtain.
eval_note: Correct if the agent reads the bedroom temperature sensor and applies the correct fan level based on the temperature range. Three-tier conditional logic. If above 32°C, should also open curtain.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Temperature-tiered fan control
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
C-->>A: temperature value
alt 25-28
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=30
C-->>A: success
else 28-32
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=60
C-->>A: success
else above 32
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=100
C-->>A: success
end
A-->>U: fan adjusted based on temperature reading

eval_note: Correct if the agent reads espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem, and espID=3 tem_out. Should calculate or approximate the average of the three indoor readings and compare to outdoor. Report only, no appliance changes unless the user asks.

A->>C: get espID=2 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem
A->>C: get espID=3 tem_out
C-->>A: all temperature values
A-->>U: report average indoor temp vs outdoor with analysis

eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) gets current datetime, (2) branches based on time: morning → web search for news; afternoon → get location + weather forecast. Should use the right tools based on the time-of-day condition.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant W as WebTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant F as WeatherTool
U->>A: Time-conditional tool usage
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current time
alt morning
A->>W: search_and_read_web_link(today news headlines)
W-->>A: results
A-->>U: summarize news

A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>F: get_hourly_forecast(location, today)
F-->>A: forecast
A-->>U: summarize weather

prompt: Search the web for the ideal indoor humidity level for tropical plants. Then check my bonsai moisture sensor. Based on the search results, tell me if my bonsai has enough moisture or needs watering.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) uses web search for ideal humidity/moisture for tropical plants, (2) reads the moisture sensor, (3) compares the actual reading to the recommended range from the search. Should provide an informed recommendation combining web knowledge and sensor data.

participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant W as WebTool
participant C as ApplianceController
U->>A: Research ideal moisture and compare with actual

W-->>A: recommended range
A->>C: get espID=3 mois
C-->>A: moisture value
A-->>U: comparison and recommendation based on research

prompt: Get my current location, then check the weather for tomorrow. Based on the forecast, add a note for tomorrow with weather-appropriate reminders (e.g., 'bring umbrella' if rain, 'use sunscreen' if sunny and hot).
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) gets current location, (2) gets current datetime to calculate tomorrow's date, (3) gets weather forecast for tomorrow, (4) adds a contextually appropriate note based on the forecast. Should use at least 3 different tools in sequence.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant N as NoteTool
U->>A: Weather forecast for tomorrow with reminder note
A->>T: get_current_datetime()
T-->>A: current date
A->>L: get_current_location()
L-->>A: location
A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(location, tomorrow)
W-->>A: forecast
alt rain expected
A->>N: add_note(bring umbrella tomorrow, [tomorrow])
else hot and sunny
A->>N: add_note(use sunscreen tomorrow, [tomorrow])
end
A-->>U: forecast summary and reminder note added

prompt: Check today's weather. If it's going to be above 35°C at any point, prepare the house for extreme heat: close all curtains, set all fans to maximum, and turn off unnecessary lights to reduce heat.
eval_note: Correct if the agent: (1) gets location and date, (2) checks weather forecast, (3) if any hour exceeds 35°C: close curtain (motor2=0), set all fans to 100%, turn off lights. Heat preparation mode with weather-conditional triggering.

sequenceDiagram
participant U as User
participant A as Agent
participant T as TimeTool
participant L as LocationTool
participant W as WeatherTool
participant C as ApplianceController

A->>L: get_current_location()

A->>W: get_hourly_forecast(location, today)

alt any hour > 35
A->>C: set espID=3 motor2=0
A->>C: set espID=1 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=2 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=3 motor1=100
A->>C: set espID=1 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led1=false
A->>C: set espID=2 led2=false
C-->>A: success
A-->>U: extreme heat mode activated
else temps moderate
A-->>U: no extreme heat expected, no changes needed

prompt: I'm about to take a nap in the bedroom. Set up the room for a comfortable nap - it should be dark, cool, and quiet.
